Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Overhaul Post-Processing (#828)

Decouple post-processing from `SearchStrategy` so that search methods
accept an explicit post-processor parameter. This enables callers to
supply custom post-processing logic (reranking, filtering, ID
translation) without defining new strategy types.

## Search trait changes

The `Search` trait now requires `S: SearchStrategy<DP, T>` at the trait
level, and introduces two new generic parameters on the `search` method:

- `O` - the output type written to the buffer (used to be a parameter of
`SearchStrategy`)
- `PP` — the post-processor, bounded by `for<'a>
SearchPostProcess<S::SearchAccessor<'a>, T, O> + Send + Sync`
- `OB` — the output buffer, bounded by `SearchOutputBuffer<O> + Send +
?Sized`

Previously, `OB` was a trait-level generic and the post-processor was
obtained internally from the strategy. Now both are method-level
generics, giving callers control over each independently.

The output "id" type `O` has been removed from `SearchStrategy`
entirely, which greatly simplifies things.

At the `DiskANNIndex` level, two entry points are provided:

- `search()` — requires `S: DefaultPostProcessor`, creates the
strategy's default processor automatically, then delegates to
`search_with()`. This is the common path.
- `search_with()` — requires only `S: SearchStrategy`, accepts any
compatible `PP`. This is the extension point for custom post-processing.

## DefaultPostProcessor

Strategies opt in to default post-processing by implementing
`DefaultPostProcessor`:

```rust
pub trait DefaultPostProcessor<Provider, T, O>: SearchStrategy<Provider, T> {
    type Processor: for<'a> SearchPostProcess<Self::SearchAccessor<'a>, T, O> + Send + Sync;
    fn default_post_processor(&self) -> Self::Processor;
}
```

The `default_post_processor!` macro covers the common case where the
processor is `Default`-constructible. `DefaultSearchStrategy` is a
convenience alias for `SearchStrategy + DefaultPostProcessor`.

## Start-point filtering

The in-mem code used to rely on a special `Internal` type to customize
post-processing for the inplace delete pipeline.
Now that post-processing is customizable, this workaround is no longer
needed.

Start-point filtering is composed at the type level using the existing
`Pipeline` mechanism:

- `HasDefaultProcessor::Processor = Pipeline<FilterStartPoints,
RemoveDeletedIdsAndCopy>` — filters start points during regular search
- `InplaceDeleteStrategy::SearchPostProcessor = RemoveDeletedIdsAndCopy`
— no start-point filtering during delete re-pruning

## Range search

To respond to moving the output buffer to a method level geneirc, range
search now writes results directly through a `DistanceFiltered` output
buffer wrapper that applies radius and inner-radius filtering inline
during post-processing. This replaces the previous approach of
allocating intermediate `Vec`s and copying results through an
`IdDistance` buffer.

`SearchOutputBuffer` is now implemented for `Vec<Neighbor<I>>`,
providing an unbounded growable buffer suitable for range search and
other cases where the result count is not known in advance.

The `RangeSearchResults` type has been removed now that a
`SearchOutputBuffer` is properly used.

## Inplace Delete
Since the post-processor is now a little decoupled from
`SearchStrategy`, rustc is unable to fully resolve the existing bounds
for the "caching" provider. Try as I might, I could not get anything to
work without adding a `DeleteSearchAccessor` associated type to
`InplaceDeleteStrategy`. At the end of the day, I think this is a
compromise we'll have to live with.

## Migration guide

**If you implement `SearchStrategy`** — minimal changes required. Remove
the `O` parfameter (if needed). Implement `DefaultPostProcessor` with
your current post-processor if you want your strategy to work with
`index.search()`. Use the `default_post_processor!` macro for simple
cases:

```rust
impl DefaultPostProcessor<MyProvider, [f32]> for MyStrategy {
    default_post_processor!(Pipeline<FilterStartPoints, RemoveDeletedIdsAndCopy>);
}
```

**If you call `index.search()`** — no API change for strategies that
implement `DefaultPostProcessor` (all built-in strategies do).

**If you need custom post-processing** — use `index.search_with()` and
pass your post-processor directly:

```rust
let processor = MyCustomPostProcessor::new(/* ... */);
let stats = index.search_with(params, &strategy, processor, &context, &query, &mut output).await?;
```

**If you implement `Search`** — the method signature has changed. `PP`
and `OB` are now method-level generics, and `processor` is an explicit
parameter:

```rust
fn search<O, PP, OB>(
    self,
    index: &DiskANNIndex<DP>,
    strategy: &S,
    processor: PP,       // new
    context: &DP::Context,
    query: &T,
    output: &mut OB,
) -> impl SendFuture<ANNResult<Self::Output>>
where
    O: Send,
    PP: for<'a> SearchPostProcess<S::SearchAccessor<'a>, T, O> + Send + Sync,
    OB: SearchOutputBuffer<O> + Send + ?Sized;
```

**If you use range search results** — `RangeSearchOutput` no longer
carries `ids` and `distances` fields. Results are written to the output
buffer passed to `search()`. Use `Vec<Neighbor<u32>>` (or any
`SearchOutputBuffer` impl) to collect them.

**If you implement `InplaceDeleteStrategy`**: Add the
`DeleteSearchAccessor` associated type, ensuring it is the same as the
accessor selected by the `SearchStrategy`. This is redundant
information, but needed to help constrain downstream trait bounds.

---------

repository.name: DiskANN

commit.message:
Retry on malformed JSON from model instead of failing immediately

Model sometimes produces invalid JSON (unescaped characters, broken
syntax). These are transient — retrying often succeeds. Removed
'parse plan' from the non-retryable error list so JSON parse errors
and missing-JSON errors trigger a retry.

feat: initial pixel-perfect MCP server

Three MCP tools for fixing, validating, and generating visual text content:
- fix: repairs broken ASCII boxes, markdown tables, progress bars
- validate: checks alignment issues, Mermaid syntax errors
- generate: routes to Mermaid/PlantUML rendering engines

Core library:
- visual_width: Unicode-aware column width calculation (emoji, CJK, ANSI)
- box_fixer: ASCII/Unicode box border alignment
- table_fixer: markdown table visual-width-aware column padding
- bar_fixer: progress bar width normalization
- mermaid_validator: regex-based Mermaid syntax validation

Copilot CLI plugin:
- SKILL.md with trigger phrases and usage instructions
- visual-content.instructions.md with prevention rules

84 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rewrite enemy visuals to use 3D geometries with distinct type designs

Replace box-based enemy models with rounded 3D geometries:
- Body: CylinderGeometry (rounded torso) instead of BoxGeometry
- Head: SphereGeometry instead of BoxGeometry
- Arms/Legs: CylinderGeometry instead of BoxGeometry
- All parts use MeshStandardMaterial with roughness/metalness
- Eyes glow red (normal) or yellow (bosses) via MeshBasicMaterial

Add detailed type-specific features for all 12 enemy types:
- zombie: hunched posture, bone protrusions, torn flesh, hanging jaw
- fast: fin crest, shoulder spikes, lean forward tilt
- tank: shoulder pauldrons, chest plate, rivets, belt
- exploding: emissive body, fuse+spark, glowing cracks, inner core
- ogre: tusks, brow ridge, chain, battle scars, spiked club
- fireDragon: snout, bone+membrane wings, dorsal spines, tail, glowing belly
- giant: crystal crown, rocky patches, rune lines, massive fists
- shield: round disc shield, boss emblem, helmet visor, armor trim
- jumping: large green eyes, spring-coil legs, webbed feet
- ranged: hood/cloak, staff with glowing orb, floating runes
- charger: forward horns, nose ring, armor plates, hoofed feet
- splitter: blobby body, internal bubbles, nucleus glow, drip tendrils

Preserves return object structure (bodyMesh, headMesh, lArm, rArm, lLeg, rLeg)
and shield userData.isShield for damage code compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Release v0.1.53

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(workflow): replace npm ci + install with single npm install for ibira.js

npm ci is structurally incompatible with this workflow: step 5 adds
ibira.js to package.json before npm ci runs, so the lock-file check
always fails on a first-time add (and on any run where the lock file
was not pre-updated). Replace both calls with a single targeted
npm install, which adds/updates ibira.js and regenerates only the
affected lock-file entries in one atomic step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Resilient tracked-path handling, --pedantic, --quiet, and file logging (#2)

* Warn instead of fail on tracked paths, add --pedantic, --quiet, and file logging

Tracked files in .worktreeinclude caused hooks to exit non-zero, breaking
Claude Code worktree creation. The default behavior now warns and skips
tracked paths (they're already in the worktree via Git). Use --pedantic
to restore the strict fail-on-tracked behavior.

Both implementations now log all output to worktree.log in the current
directory with timestamps and levels. --quiet suppresses stderr while
keeping the log file, useful for hook mode debugging.

Tests: 98 assertions (was 86), covering warn-on-tracked, --pedantic,
and --quiet for both Python and Bash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review: log file validation, test isolation, README clarity

- --quiet + unwritable log file is now fatal (both implementations),
  preventing silent output loss
- test_create_quiet runs from a dedicated temp cwd and asserts
  worktree.log exists with content
- README gotcha clarifies the tracked-path behavior changed in v0.3
  (was error, now warn+skip by default)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

commit.message:
feat(ax): scaffold treatment-v6 condition with CodeSeeker MCP

Adds v6 experiment condition with three key deltas from v5:
- CodeSeeker v2.0.0 MCP activated (search-first rule + DRY gate)
- §8 DRY gate added to Verification Protocol
- §9 Interface Completeness gate added to Verification Protocol
- ESLint config emitted in P0 as P1 enforcement gate

Full COI disclosure: CodeSeeker is author's own GS-built tool.
v6 tests GS ecosystem compounding as an orthogonal dimension.

commit.message:
Add compatibility tests for spherical flatbuffer serialization (#839)

Check in and run compatibility tests for the flatbuffer serialized
Spherical quantizers.

Baselines are generated using the inline test dataset in `iface.rs` and
capture the serialized representation of the quantizer as well as the
bit-patterns for compressed vectors and all pairwise distances among the
dataset.

These should cover the following:
* Bit Width: 1, 2, 4, and 8.
* Metric: `SquaredL2`, `InnerProduct` and `Cosine`.
* PreScale: None and `ReciprocalMeanNorm`

Baseline files are checked in as JSON files to
`diskann-quantization/test/generated/spherical`. They can be regenerated
by setting the `DISKANN_TEST=overwrite` environment variable.

Additionally, this adds tests for the Cosine metric to the existing
serialization tests.

Due to test failures on macos, the `Check` enum for absolute/relative
tolerance checking has been promoted to
`diskann_quantization::test_util`.

---------

Co-authored-by: Mark Hildebrand <mhildebrand@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Graph-structured Indices for Scalable, Fast, Fresh and Filtered Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search

commit.message:
Add troubleshooting note for dbt debug logs in `ExecutionMode.WATCHER` (#2491)

Documents that dbt debug logs can be enabled by passing `--debug` via
`dbt_cmd_global_flags` in `operator_args`, and that
`AIRFLOW__LOGGING__LOGGING_LEVEL=DEBUG` must be set to make them visible
when running dbt as a subprocess.

Relates to: https://github.com/astronomer/astronomer-cosmos/issues/2317

commit.message:
docs(white-paper): adversarial audit fixes — 17 findings addressed

FATAL fixes:
- F1: Named circularity disclosure added as explicit threat; external
  metrics (tsc/ESLint/npm audit) named as partial mitigation; third-party
  tool validation named as planned post-submission step
- F2/F10: Theoretical S vs S_realized proxy distinction added to §9.4;
  S_realized is product instrumentation, not independent measurement
- F3: Specification-authorship confound named explicitly; 'no other
  practitioner has applied GS as of this submission' stated directly
- F4: Post-hoc progression labeled 'iterated optimization on a single
  benchmark, not independent confirmation' in §7.7.B.1 lead

SIGNIFICANT fixes:
- F5: Chomsky analogy disclaimer strengthened; LLMs do not formally
  instantiate Type 1 grammars; formal apparatus not imported; intended
  for linguists to read without objection
- F6: §7.7.B.1 lead now explicitly states control beat GS v1 on
  Executable; GS v1 advantage was Composable only
- F7: 'independently reproduced' -> 'reproduced in a separate
  author-run session' (abstract)
- F8: Paradigm contingency nuance added; what may dissolve with stronger
  models vs what is system-level and survives
- F9: Morris inversion acknowledged; GS uses Morris as taxonomy while
  inverting its directionality
- F10: See F2/F10
- F11: AI auditor explicitly named; no inter-rater reliability disclosed
- F12: 'cannot regress' -> 'tends toward rising floor' with quality gate
  correctness caveat; closing spiral framing

MODERATE fixes:
- F13: 1957 claim reframed as retrospective observation, not established
  historiographic fact
- F14: Mutation gap named as GS v1 specification failure (constraint
  absent from template), not model failure
- F15: 'zero application code' claim given same caveat as active-hours
  (self-reported, git history records commits not authorship)
- F16: Domain dimensional expansion labeled as hypothesis with
  observational evidence; controlled experiment named as proposed
  follow-up; meta-ask inversion noted
- F17: Ceiling decimal values replaced with ordinal bands; S_aggregate
  quantitative prediction removed; false precision eliminated

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add delegate mode — helm as Copilot orchestrator

New 'delegate' mode where helm enriches issues with project context
(language, build commands, SOT, file tree, manifests) and assigns
them to Copilot coding agent instead of generating code directly.

- agent.mode = 'delegate' in helm.toml or --delegate flag
- Context comment with structured markdown and <details> sections
- Assigns issue to @copilot via gh issue edit
- Daemon logs warning when running in delegate mode
- Human-friendly output shows delegated status and next steps
- Direct mode (current behavior) remains the default

Copilot works inside a Codespace and can build/test/iterate,
making this suitable for complex codebases where single-shot
Models API generation falls short.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix first character missing by waiting for modifier key release

Replace fixed 300ms delay with active polling via GetAsyncKeyState
that waits until Ctrl/Shift/Alt are actually released before typing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(settings): add decimal mode, display 2 decimals and store values x100 (#30)

* Initial plan

* feat: implement decimal mode in advanced settings

Add a new 'Decimal mode' setting (disabled by default) in Advanced Settings.
When enabled:
- Stored values remain integers but represent cents (×100)
- UI displays 2 decimal places with dot separator (e.g. 125 → '1.25')
- Input accepts both ',' and '.' as decimal separators
- TPV push-from-right: typing '125' = 1.25; typing '1.25' = 1.25
- Fixed -1/-2 buttons deduct 1.00/2.00 (=100/200 stored units)
- Button labels update to '-1.00'/'-2.00' in decimal mode
- Before/after, actual balance, and transaction history formatted accordingly

Co-authored-by: duhow <1145001+duhow@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/duhow/nfc-coins/sessions/dbad33b1-d9e7-4558-a7e0-9e4732f96dc0

* refactor: unify duplicate string keys in strings.xml

- Remove unused `balance_format` (%d) — never referenced in code or layouts
- Remove `setting_static_key_hint` — identical to `setting_static_key`, unused
- Merge `deduct_ok_decimal` into `deduct_ok` (update to %s format)
- Merge `balance_added_ok_decimal` into `balance_added_ok` (update to %s format)
- Simplify corresponding if/else branches in MainActivity to single calls

Co-authored-by: duhow <1145001+duhow@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/duhow/nfc-coins/sessions/d23f8918-dcd5-49ac-9bb9-01b609dcee1d

* fix: decimal mode comma input, button truncation, integer display

- afterTextChanged: replace early return with raw update after comma→dot
  normalization so the balance display updates immediately on comma input
- applyTheme: reduce button text size to 20sp in decimal mode so "-1.00"
  and "-2.00" fit within the 100dp button width without truncation
- formatBalanceDisplay: omit decimal part when value % 100 == 0 (e.g.
  100 stored → "1", not "1.00") for cleaner whole-number display
- openAddBalanceDialog: use formatBalanceDisplay(0) instead of
  hardcoded "+0.00" for consistency with the new integer display rule

Co-authored-by: duhow <1145001+duhow@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/duhow/nfc-coins/sessions/cab20f58-9107-4c17-83ad-4d7cdfcfcb90

* fix: revert integer display, fix comma keyboard input via DigitsKeyListener

- formatBalanceDisplay: revert the 'value % 100 == 0' shortcut; always
  render as "%d.%02d" in decimal mode to avoid confusion while typing
- etHiddenInput: replace inputType= assignment with keyListener +
  setRawInputType so that comma (",") is accepted by the input field
  (DigitsKeyListener allows it) while the numeric keyboard is preserved
  (setRawInputType forces TYPE_CLASS_NUMBER). The existing comma→dot
  normalization in afterTextChanged then handles it correctly.

Co-authored-by: duhow <1145001+duhow@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/duhow/nfc-coins/sessions/cea4829c-9768-4bf0-9e58-2389b9a4353a

* Update strings.xml

docs(white-paper): three structural improvements — no DX required

(a) Diagram relocation: Figure 1 (Domain Stack) moved from end of
    §4.1.c to immediately after the two-axes paragraph in §4.1.a,
    before §4.1.b. Now appears the moment the Chomsky/Martin axes
    are first described, not after two subsections that reference
    the concept.

(b) §7.7.B insight-first: added three-sentence lead paragraph at
    the top of §7.7.B.1 Results naming the three main findings
    upfront (naive-vs-structured boundary, 14/14 achievability,
    experiment-corrects-itself property). Tables and details now
    read as evidence for a stated story, not a puzzle to decode.

(c) About Author elevation: 'every trade at once' passage moved from
    last paragraph to first, so the section opens with the story
    that explains the paper rather than burying it after credentials.
    Doctoral speculation moved to closing position.

commit.message:
Change default model from gpt-4o to gpt-4.1

gpt-4o truncates JSON output on longer code generation tasks.
gpt-4.1 has a larger output token limit and handles full-page
content generation reliably. Also add max_tokens: 16384 to the
API request.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(white-paper): adversarial audit fixes — theorem→principle, pre-registration, FC COI, formula frame, Executable, BRAD taxonomy, supplement URL, Invellum scope, domain dimensional expansion

- Rename 'community convergence theorem' → 'community convergence principle' throughout (theorem requires formal proof; this is a structural argument)
- Replace 'pre-registered' with 'prospectively designed, verified by timestamped commits' + add repo URL
- Add ForgeCraft COI scope clarification: all 6 cases predate FC or used pre-release; methodology validity independent of the tool
- Reframe I∝(1-S)/S from 'deductive claim' → 'analytically derived theoretical frame'; add note that DX will collect left-side data (iteration counts per participant)
- Unify Executable emergence framing: 'implicit from beginning, articulated as formal property by the experiment'
- Add GS_Experiment_Supplement.md availability URL at first citation
- Qualify Invellum metrics table as qualitative case — no pre-specification baseline available
- Update BRAD taxonomy from vague 'AI-derived' to named 3-tier structure: 12 event types, 8 legal relevance tags, 7 behavioral pattern tags; jurisdiction-aware (MN statutes + all 50 states); statutory grounding noted
- Add 'Domain dimensional expansion' to glossary with full definition

commit.message:
build: ship README.txt and QUICK-START.txt in release zip, not .md

- Copy README.md as README.txt in distribution package so users
  opening the zip see plain text files, not .md files
- Rewrite QUICK-START.txt inline content: fix wrong exe name
  (GA-WsusManager -> WsusManager), add SQL installer prereqs,
  add Air-Gap Transfer section, GPO Deployment section, Schedule
  section, keyboard shortcuts, common issues table, v4.0.3/4.0.4
  auto-decline/approve rules

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Resume from existing branch instead of failing

When a branch already exists for an issue:
- If a PR is already open: error with a link to the existing PR
- If no PR exists: check out the existing branch, regenerate code,
  commit on top, and force-push with lease

This handles the case where a previous run failed mid-way (e.g.,
after branching but before pushing). The agent picks up where it
left off instead of blocking.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(architecture-mismatch): document coverage/ and dist/ subdirs in ARCHITECTURE.md

- Add coverage/ and coverage/lcov-report/ to directory tree
- Add dist/ subtree (dist/src/, dist/esm/, dist/esm/types/,
  dist/types/, dist/test/) to directory tree
- Update output targets prose to match actual build layout

commit.message:
Release v0.1.50

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Gracefully handle missing board statuses, auto-create in upgrade

- project start: warn instead of error when board status (In Progress,
  In Review) is missing — PR creation continues regardless
- upgrade/doctor --fix: detect missing board statuses (Ready,
  In Progress, In Review, Done) and add them via GraphQL API
- New github.FetchBoardStatuses() and AddBoardStatuses() helpers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add GitHub Models link to generated helm.toml

Generated config files now include a header comment linking to
https://github.com/marketplace/models so users can find valid
model names. Also updated helm.toml.example.
